div.style: display: inline-block; background-color: #d9e0e7; padding: 6px; margin: 4px; border: 1px solid #8da1b4; border-radius: 5px; line-height: 60%

table.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; font-size: 11px; font-family: verdana; vertical-align: top;

tr.style: border: 1px #F0F0F0 solid; font-size: 11px; font-family: verdana; vertical-align: top

th.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; padding-top: 3px; padding-bottom: 3px

a.title: The logical name of the element

th.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; padding-top: 3px; padding-bottom: 3px

a.title: Information about the use of the element

th.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; padding-top: 3px; padding-bottom: 3px

a.title: Minimum and Maximum # of times the element can appear in the instance

th.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; padding-top: 3px; padding-bottom: 3px; width: 100px

a.title: Reference to the type of the element

th.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; padding-top: 3px; padding-bottom: 3px

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: white

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; white-space: nowrap; background-image: url(tbl_bck1.png)
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
td.span: Instances of this logical model can be the target of a Reference

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: #F7F7F7

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; white-space: nowrap; background-image: url(tbl_bck11.png)
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

span.style: padding-left: 3px; padding-right: 3px; color: white; background-color: red
span.title: This element has obligations

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: white

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; white-space: nowrap; background-image: url(tbl_bck110.png)
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

span.style: padding-left: 3px; padding-right: 3px; color: white; background-color: red
span.title: This element has obligations

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: #F7F7F7

span.style: padding-left: 3px; padding-right: 3px; color: white; background-color: red
span.title: This element has obligations

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: white

span.style: padding-left: 3px; padding-right: 3px; color: white; background-color: red
span.title: This element has obligations

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
td.span: Author(s) by whom the laboratory result report was authored.

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: #F7F7F7

span.title: This element has obligations

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: #F7F7F7

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: white

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; white-space: nowrap; background-image: url(tbl_bck110.png)

vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
td.span: Type of document at hand, e.g. 60591-5 Patient summary document.

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: #F7F7F7

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; white-space: nowrap; background-image: url(tbl_bck110.png)

vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
td.span: Human readable document title that can be displayed in search results, etc. This can be documentType's display name, or it can be assembled from multiple elements. Examples: 'Laboratory Result Report', 'Patient Summary of Jane Green 10.12.2024'

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: white

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: #F7F7F7

td.span: Categorisation of the event covered by the document (e.g. laboratory study types, imaging study types including modality, etc.). Selection of such tags or labels depends on the use case and agreement between data sharing parties. This meta-data element serves primarily for searching and filtering purposes.

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: white

td.span: The person/organisation "authorised" to place the order. Order placer could be either a health professional, health professional organisation or the patient himself.

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: #F7F7F7

td.span: An explanation or justification for why this service is being requested in coded form.

vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
td.span: Specification of clinical question (goal of the investigation) to be answered by the laboratory investigation.

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: #F7F7F7

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; white-space: nowrap; background-image: url(tbl_bck111.png)

span.title: This element has obligations

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
td.span: Additional clinical information about the patient or specimen that may influence the services or their interpretations. This information includes diagnosis, clinical findings and other observations. In laboratory ordering these are typically referred to as 'ask at order entry questions (AOEs).' This includes observations explicitly requested by the producer (filler) to provide context or supporting information needed to complete the order. For example, reporting the amount of inspired oxygen for blood gas measurements.

td.span: A Sex Parameter for Clinical Use is a parameter that provides guidance on how a recipient should apply settings or reference ranges that are derived from observable information such as an organ inventory, recent hormone lab tests, genetic testing, menstrual status, obstetric history, etc.. This property is intended for use in clinical decision making, and indicates that treatment or diagnostic tests should consider best practices associated with the relevant reference population

description.value: Obligations for the logical model of laboratory report

short.value: Laboratory report model

definition.value: Laboratory result report model.

valueMarkdown.value: Each producer of a laboratory report SHALL be able to provide basic information, such as type of the report, authorship, status of the report, title, etc. This information is crucial for safe identification of the report and for searchability of the report using standardized searching parameters.
EHR systems that consume laboratory report SHALL process document header information.

short.value: Laboratory Report header

definition.value: Basic metadata for this information

valueMarkdown.value: EHR systems SHALL provide information about the subject of care.
EHR system SHALL render information about the subject of care in such a way that unambiguous assignment to the care subject is preserved.
Information about the subject of care SHALL be processed by the EHR system in such a way that the identity of the subject of care cannot be confused.

short.value: Patient/subject information

definition.value: Patient/subject information

short.value: Unique identifier of the document

definition.value: Business identifier for the object, unique within its system. Supporting disambiguation between different contexts (systems/countries).

short.value: Author(s) by whom the laboratory result report was authored.

definition.value: Author(s) by whom the laboratory result report was authored. Multiple authors could be provided.

comment.value: Author of the laboratory report could be either a health professional who authored the report, laboratory itself or laboratory information system. Author information is the only mandatory element of the laboratory report header. As such, each laboratory shall be able to provide at least the author information.

short.value: Date of finalising/issuing the document

definition.value: Date and optionally time of authoring/issuing

short.value: Status of the document

definition.value: Status of the resource or document

short.value: Source of information (e.g. patient, patient's proxy). Used in case the patient has authored the information or the author has submitted patient-stated information.

definition.value: Source of information (e.g. patient, patient's proxy). Used in case the patient has authored the information or the author has submitted patient-stated information.

short.value: Language in which the resource is written. Language is expressed by the IETF language tag.

definition.value: Language in which the resource is written. Language is expressed by the IETF language tag.

short.value: Type of document at hand, e.g. 60591-5 Patient summary document.

definition.value: Type of document at hand, e.g. 60591-5 Patient summary document.

short.value: Human readable document title that can be displayed in search results, etc. This can be documentType's display name, or it can be assembled from multiple elements. Examples: 'Laboratory Result Report', 'Patient Summary of Jane Green 10.12.2024'

definition.value: Human readable document title that can be displayed in search results, etc. This can be documentType's display name, or it can be assembled from multiple elements. Examples: 'Laboratory Result Report', 'Patient Summary of Jane Green 10.12.2024'

short.value: Time of service that is being documented

definition.value: Time of service that is being documented

short.value: Business version of the document. The exact algorithm for versioning is decided by the producer/custodian

definition.value: Business version of the document. The exact algorithm for versioning is decided by the producer/custodian

short.value: Document attestation details

definition.value: Document attestation details

short.value: Attester who validated the document

definition.value: Attester who validated laboratory result report. Multiple attesters could be provided.

comment.value: Attestation (validation) of the laboratory report content is the usual step before legal authentication. Attestation could be provided by e.g. the laboratory specialist or the laboratory information system based on internal rules of the laboratory.

short.value: Date and time of the approval of the document by Attester

definition.value: Date and time of the approval of the document by Attester

short.value: Document legal authentication details

definition.value: Document legal authentication details

short.value: The person or organisation taking responsibility for the content of the document.

definition.value: The person or organization taking responsibility for the medical content of the document

comment.value: Legal authentication of the laboratory report is the usual step after attestation. Legal authentication could be provided by e.g. the head of the laboratory or the laboratory organization itself.

short.value: Date and time when the document was authorised

definition.value: Date and time when the document was authorised

short.value: Categorisation of the event covered by the document (e.g. laboratory study types, imaging study types including modality, etc.). Selection of such tags or labels depends on the use case and agreement between data sharing parties. This meta-data element serves primarily for searching and filtering purposes.

definition.value: Categorisation of the event covered by the document (e.g. laboratory study types, imaging study types including modality, etc.). Selection of such tags or labels depends on the use case and agreement between data sharing parties. This meta-data element serves primarily for searching and filtering purposes.

short.value: Additional details about where the content was created (e.g. clinical specialty)

definition.value: Additional details about where the content was created (e.g. clinical specialty)

short.value: Organisation that is in charge of maintaining the document/report.

definition.value: Organisation that is in charge of maintaining the document/report.

short.value: Intended recipient

definition.value: Information recipient (intended recipient or recipients of the report, additional recipients might be identified by the ordering party, e.g. GP, other specialist), if applicable

valueMarkdown.value: Each EHR system that is a producer of a laboratory report SHALL be able to provide the entire report as issued by the laboratory in a human readable format, e.g. PDF.
EHR systems that consume laboratory report SHALL process the human readable version of the report.
EHR systems that consume laboratory report SHALL render human readable version of the report to the endusers.

short.value: A narrative easy-to-read representation of the full data set, e.g. PDF-version of a document

definition.value: A narrative easy-to-read representation of the full data set, e.g. PDF-version of a document

valueMarkdown.value: Each EHR system that is a producer of a laboratory report SHALL be able to provide structured information about the laboratory investigation, such as order information, supporting information, specimen details and result data.
EHR systems that consume laboratory report SHALL process structured body information.

short.value: Laboratory Report structured body

definition.value: Laboratory Report structured body

valueMarkdown.value: EHR systems SHALL be able to populate information about one or more order which this report is based on.
Consumer EHR systems SHALL display key order information to ensure traceability between order and report.

short.value: Order Information (Laboratory Result Report could respond to multiple test orders)

definition.value: Order Information (Laboratory Result Report could respond to multiple test orders)

valueMarkdown.value: Consumer systems SHOULD use this element to pair result report with laboratory order.

short.value: An identifier of the laboratory test order. Laboratory Result Report may respond to multiple orders.

definition.value: An identifier of the laboratory test order. Laboratory Result Report may respond to multiple orders.

short.value: Date and time of the order placement.

definition.value: Date and time of the order placement.

short.value: The person/organisation "authorised" to place the order. Order placer could be either a health professional, health professional organisation or the patient himself.

definition.value: The person/organisation "authorised" to place the order. Order placer could be either a health professional, health professional organisation or the patient himself.

short.value: An explanation or justification for why this service is being requested in coded form.

definition.value: An explanation or justification for why this service is being requested in coded form.

short.value: Specification of clinical question (goal of the investigation) to be answered by the laboratory investigation.

definition.value: Specification of clinical question (goal of the investigation) to be answered by the laboratory investigation.

short.value: Additional clinical information about the patient or specimen that may influence the services or their interpretations. This information includes diagnosis, clinical findings and other observations. In laboratory ordering these are typically referred to as 'ask at order entry questions (AOEs).' This includes observations explicitly requested by the producer (filler) to provide context or supporting information needed to complete the order. For example, reporting the amount of inspired oxygen for blood gas measurements.

definition.value: Additional clinical information about the patient or specimen that may influence the services or their interpretations. This information includes diagnosis, clinical findings and other observations. In laboratory ordering these are typically referred to as 'ask at order entry questions (AOEs).' This includes observations explicitly requested by the producer (filler) to provide context or supporting information needed to complete the order. For example, reporting the amount of inspired oxygen for blood gas measurements.

short.value: Clinical findings and other observations.

definition.value: Clinical findings and other observations.

short.value: Condition that may influence the service or result interpretation.

definition.value: Condition that may influence the service or result interpretation.

short.value: Medication administered before ordering the service.

definition.value: Medication administered before ordering the service.

short.value: A Sex Parameter for Clinical Use is a parameter that provides guidance on how a recipient should apply settings or reference ranges that are derived from observable information such as an organ inventory, recent hormone lab tests, genetic testing, menstrual status, obstetric history, etc.. This property is intended for use in clinical decision making, and indicates that treatment or diagnostic tests should consider best practices associated with the relevant reference population

definition.value: A Sex Parameter for Clinical Use is a parameter that provides guidance on how a recipient should apply settings or reference ranges that are derived from observable information such as an organ inventory, recent hormone lab tests, genetic testing, menstrual status, obstetric history, etc.. This property is intended for use in clinical decision making, and indicates that treatment or diagnostic tests should consider best practices associated with the relevant reference population

short.value: Vaccination history of the patient.

definition.value: Vaccination history of the patient.

short.value: Any other type of relevant supporting information

definition.value: Any other type of relevant supporting information

short.value: Specimen information

definition.value: Specimen information

short.value: Specification of requested service or services

definition.value: Specification of requested service or services

short.value: Laboratory report result data

definition.value: Laboratory report result data

short.value: Narrative Comments, such as a textual interpretation or advice accompanying the result report, for example.

definition.value: Narrative Comments, such as a textual interpretation or advice accompanying the result report, for example.

short.value: Observation details (report could consist of multiple observations)

definition.value: Observation details (report could consist of multiple observations)

valueMarkdown.value: Each EHR system that is a producer of a laboratory report SHOULD be able to provide attachments related to the report, such as documents, diagrams, images of the results, etc.
EHR systems that consume laboratory report SHOULD process attachments related to the report.
EHR systems that consume laboratory report SHOULD render attachments related to the report to the endusers.

short.value: Report attachments

definition.value: Report attachments

valueMarkdown.value: Each producer of a laboratory report SHALL be able to provide basic information, such as type of the report, authorship, status of the report, title, etc. This information is crucial for safe identification of the report and for searchability of the report using standardized searching parameters.
EHR systems that consume laboratory report SHALL process document header information.
EHR systems SHALL provide information about the subject of care.
EHR system SHALL render information about the subject of care in such a way that unambiguous assignment to the care subject is preserved.
Information about the subject of care SHALL be processed by the EHR system in such a way that the identity of the subject of care cannot be confused.
Each EHR system that is a producer of a laboratory report SHALL be able to provide the entire report as issued by the laboratory in a human readable format, e.g. PDF.
EHR systems that consume laboratory report SHALL process the human readable version of the report.
EHR systems that consume laboratory report SHALL render human readable version of the report to the endusers.

Each EHR system that is a producer of a laboratory report SHALL be able to provide structured information about the laboratory investigation, such as order information, supporting information, specimen details and result data.
EHR systems that consume laboratory report SHALL process structured body information.
EHR systems SHALL be able to populate information about one or more order which this report is based on.
Consumer EHR systems SHALL display key order information to ensure traceability between order and report.
Consumer systems SHOULD use this element to pair result report with laboratory order.
Each EHR system that is a producer of a laboratory report SHOULD be able to provide attachments related to the report, such as documents, diagrams, images of the results, etc.
EHR systems that consume laboratory report SHOULD process attachments related to the report.
EHR systems that consume laboratory report SHOULD render attachments related to the report to the endusers.
